IN THE DISTRICT COURT OF CENTRAL CITY  CASE NO.: CC-2024-CV-07831  PLAINTIFF: DOWNTOWN FITNESS CENTER MANAGEMENT GROUP  DEFENDANT: EMILY CARTER  DATE OF HEARING: OCTOBER 17, 2024  PRESIDING JUDGE: HON. REBECCA MARTINEZ  COURT REPORTER: LINDA PETERSON  --- CASE SUMMARY ---  Defendant Emily Carter (DOB: 1992-05-15), a professional Graphic Designer residing at 214 Oak Street, Central City, is alleged to have violated Section 7.2 of Downtown Fitness Center's membership agreement. The dispute centers on Ms. Carter's use of photography equipment within facility premises during peak hours on September 12, 2024.  --- FACTUAL BACKGROUND ---  1. Defendant has maintained a Platinum membership at Downtown Fitness Center since January 2023, with regular attendance documented on Tuesdays/Thursdays between 6-8 PM.  2. On September 12, 2024, at approximately 7:15 PM, facility staff observed Ms. Carter utilizing a professional DSLR camera in the cardio zone. When questioned, she stated she was capturing "architectural details" for her personal photography hobby portfolio.  3. Plaintiff entered into evidence three (3) photographs timestamped 7:08-7:14 PM showing identifiable members mid-workout. Membership records confirm Ms. Carter signed the facility's photography waiver on 2023-01-15 prohibiting member photography without written consent.  --- DEFENDANT TESTIMONY ---  Ms. Carter testified under oath: "As a Graphic Designer, I study spatial composition. These shots focused solely on equipment arrangements for my photography hobby. My hiking hobby involves nature photography - this was purely artistic study. I obscured all faces during editing."  --- PLAINTIFF EVIDENCE ---  • Security footage showing defendant adjusting camera angles toward occupied elliptical machines • Signed membership agreement Section 7.2: "No image capture devices permitted in activity zones" • Complaint statements from two members appearing in background of Exhibit B  --- COURT FINDINGS ---  1. Defendant's birthdate (1992-05-15) and occupation (Graphic Designer) verified through state ID  2. The Downtown Fitness Center location was properly identified as the incident site  3. Defendant's hobbies (photography/hiking) corroborated by her Instagram profile @ECarterDesign  --- VERDICT ---  The Court finds Defendant violated contractual terms but without malicious intent. RULING: 1) $250 civil penalty 2) Permanent photography ban at facility 3) Mandatory deletion of all images containing members  JUDGMENT ENTERED THIS 20TH DAY OF OCTOBER, 2024  /s/ Hon. Rebecca Martinez District Court Judge

text: UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT CENTRAL DISTRICT OF ILLINOIS  CASE NO.: 1:23-cv-04567  EMILY CARTER, Plaintiff, v. CREATIVE DESIGNS CO., Defendant.  ==========================================  COURT MINUTE ENTRY  Before: Hon. Margaret A. Bennett Date: November 15, 2023 Time: 10:00 AM Courtroom: 6B  ==========================================  PARTIES PRESENT: - Plaintiff EMILY CARTER, Pro Se   Occupation: Graphic Designer   Contact: emily.carter84@outlook.com   Address: 4512 Maple Avenue, Apt 302, Springfield, IL 62704  - Defense Counsel: Mr. Daniel R. Thorne (Smith & Weston LLP)  ==========================================  PROCEEDINGS SUMMARY: This hearing addressed Defendant's Motion to Dismiss in copyright infringement case 23-cv-04567. Plaintiff Emily Carter, a freelance graphic designer, alleges unauthorized commercial use of her digital artwork by Creative Designs Co. since June 2023.  Ms. Carter presented evidence including dated project files and client correspondence. Defense argued independent creation, citing internal design logs. The Court noted substantial similarity in contested assets and denied dismissal, citing triable factual disputes under 17 U.S.C. § 501.  DISCOVERY SCHEDULE ORDERED: 1. Defendant to produce internal design records by December 15, 2023 2. Plaintiff to provide deposition dates via email (emily.carter84@outlook.com) 3. Settlement conference set for January 19, 2024  NEXT HEARING: Status Conference – February 2, 2024, 9:30 AM  SO ORDERED.  /s/ Hon. Margaret A. Bennett United States District Judge Dated: November 15, 2023

text: **IN THE CIRCUIT COURT OF SANGAMON COUNTY, ILLINOIS**  **Case No.:** SC-2023-CV-04571 **Presiding Judge:** Hon. Margaret T. Bennett **Date of Hearing:** November 14, 2023 **Parties:** - **Plaintiff:** Emily Carter - **Defendant:** PixelPerfect Media LLC  ---  **I. CASE BACKGROUND** Plaintiff Emily Carter (DOB: August 22, 1985), a professional Graphic Designer residing at 123 Maple Street, Apt 4B, Springfield, IL 62704, filed a breach-of-contract claim against PixelPerfect Media LLC. The dispute arose from unpaid freelance design services rendered between January–March 2023. Plaintiff asserted she delivered all assets per agreement but received only 40% of the $8,500 fee. Key communications occurred via plaintiff's email: emily.carter84@outlook.com.  **II. EVIDENCE PRESENTED** - Plaintiff submitted signed contracts, project deliverables, and email chains showing repeated payment demands. - Defendant countered with claims of "incomplete branding guidelines," though failed to provide contractual benchmarks for rejection. - Plaintiff testified: "As a sole proprietor, non-payment jeopardized my livelihood. PixelPerfect ignored six invoices post-delivery."  **III. COURT FINDINGS** 1. Contract validity established via authenticated emails from emily.carter84@outlook.com. 2. Defendant's non-payment constituted material breach; no evidence supported claims of deficient work. 3. Plaintiff demonstrated documented income loss tied to occupation as Graphic Designer.  **IV. JUDGMENT** Defendant ordered to: 1. Pay $5,100 outstanding balance + $1,200 late penalties. 2. Cover court costs ($320).  Final ruling entered November 14, 2023. Full compliance required by December 15, 2023.  --- **FILED:** Sangamon County Clerk's Office | Springfield, IL **ELECTRONICALLY SIGNED:** Hon. Margaret T. Bennett

text: UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE DISTRICT OF OREGON  CASE NO. 3:24-cv-01208  PLAINTIFF: EMILY CARTER Occupation: Graphic Designer Employer: Creative Studios Inc. Work Address: 123 Maple Street, Portland, OR 97205  DEFENDANT: INNOVATE MEDIA GROUP LLC  **COURT REPORT: CARTER v. INNOVATE MEDIA GROUP**  **I. CASE OVERVIEW** This copyright infringement case (filed April 12, 2024) involves Plaintiff Emily Carter, a professional graphic designer employed at Creative Studios Inc., 123 Maple Street, Portland, OR. Ms. Carter holds no criminal record. Defendant Innovate Media Group LLC is accused of unauthorized commercial use of original vector illustrations created by Ms. Carter.  **II. FACTUAL BACKGROUND** Between June-October 2023, Ms. Carter developed a series of Pacific Northwest-themed illustrations during her employment at Creative Studios Inc. In February 2024, she identified these designs prominently featured in Innovate Media Group's national advertising campaign without license or attribution.  **III. PROCEEDINGS (May 15, 2024 Hearing)** - Plaintiff testimony established Ms. Carter created the works as part of her graphic design duties at Creative Studios Inc. Digital timestamps and workplace server logs corroborated authorship. - Defense argued independent creation but failed to produce preliminary sketches or dated source files. - Court noted Defendant's campaign launched 11 months after Ms. Carter's dated creations.  **IV. EVIDENCE** - Plaintiff Exhibits: Original .AI design files, Creative Studios Inc. internal project records, dated employment contracts. - Criminal Background Check: Confirmed Emily Carter has no criminal record in Oregon or federal databases.  **V. JUDGMENT** Finding willful infringement (August 1, 2024): 1. Permanent injunction against Defendant's use of designs 2. Statutory damages: $35,000 3. Attorney fees: $18,400  **VI. POST-JUDGMENT** Defendant complied with takedown order by September 5, 2024. Final settlement disbursed to Ms. Carter via Creative Studios Inc.'s legal department on October 10, 2024.  // REPORT END // Clerk's Note: Electronic filing certified by Deputy Clerk J. Alvarez (D. Oregon)

text: UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA  CASE NO.: 1:23-cv-02817 JAMES WILSON, Plaintiff v. EMILY CARTER, Defendant  COURT REPORT: HEARING ON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT  I. HEARING DETAILS Date: November 15, 2023 Presiding: Hon. Margaret A. Bennett Court Reporter: Lisa Rodriguez  II. DEFENDANT PROFILE Name: Emily Carter DOB: April 22, 1985 (Age: 38) Residence: Washington, D.C. Occupation: Landscape Architect Family Relations: - Spouse: Michael Carter - Child: Sophia Carter (Age 8) - Mother: Linda Thompson  III. CASE BACKGROUND Civil Action: Property damage dispute (Wilson v. Carter). Plaintiff alleges Defendant's negligent garden redesign caused flooding damage to adjacent property. Defendant maintains all work complied with municipal codes.  IV. DEFENDANT'S BACKGROUND ASSESSMENT - Criminal Record: Verified no prior criminal history - Personal Affidavit Notes:   "Gardening is my passion, but I always follow regulations. My downtime involves reading mystery novels – ironically, I never imagined being in one." - Character Witnesses: Spouse (Michael Carter) and Mother (Linda Thompson) present in gallery.  V. PROCEEDINGS SUMMARY Defense argued Plaintiff failed to provide engineering reports substantiating causation. Plaintiff countered with photographic evidence. Court noted Defendant's clean record and professional background in horticulture. Defendant testified: "My design included proper drainage channels; heavy rainfall was the proximate cause."  VI. RULING Motion for Summary Judgment DENIED. Case proceeds to trial. Next conference scheduled January 10, 2024. Electronic discovery ordered.  VII. ADDENDUM Defendant observed reviewing botanical documents during recess. Bailiff noted family members providing support throughout proceedings.  [Report Filed: November 16, 2023 | Clerk: Robert Chen]
